sections: What Our LAPADA Membership Means for West Sussex Collectors

When you trust someone to move a Georgian bureau, a longcase clock or an oil painting that has hung in the same drawing room for generations, accreditation matters. As a LAPADA member — LAPADA being the Association of Art & Antiques Dealers — we operate to the standards the antiques trade itself expects. That membership is not a badge we bought; it reflects how we pack, lift, transport and account for valuable and fragile items every working week across West Sussex. Dealers, auctioneers and private collectors recognise the LAPADA name, which is precisely why so many of them ask us to handle their consignments.

Membership of a recognised trade association sits alongside our other credentials. We are Checkatrade-verified, fully insured with £10m public liability cover, and we have been a family-run Sussex removals & storage business since 2016, based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough. For antiques in particular, that combination of trade recognition and genuine local roots gives clients confidence that their pieces will be handled by people who understand both the financial and the sentimental value of what they are carrying. You can read more about our background and accreditations on our about us page, or explore the detail of our specialised antiques moving service.
Handling Clocks, Furniture, Ceramics, Paintings & Mirrors

Antiques are not a single category — each type of piece carries its own vulnerabilities, and the right method for one would damage another. A barometer or a longcase clock, for instance, needs its movement, pendulum and weights removed and packed separately before the case is wrapped, never moved upright with the works swinging inside. A marble-topped washstand must have its top lifted off and carried flat on its edge, because marble fractures under its own weight when laid face-down. We approach every item with the specific handling its construction and condition demand.

Across a typical West Sussex collection we routinely deal with:

  • Clocks & barometers — movements, pendulums, weights and glass dials detached and packed individually, mercury columns kept vertical and secured
  • Period furniture — veneers, marquetry, loose joints and original glass protected with soft-wrap before any crate or blanket goes near them
  • Ceramics & glass — figurines, vases and dinner services individually wrapped, cushioned and boxed so no two pieces touch
  • Paintings & framed works — corners protected, glazing taped, canvases carried face-to-face and crated upright, never stacked flat
  • Mirrors & gilt frames — gilding handled with gloved hands to avoid skin oils, glass face-protected and edge-padded against vibration in transit

Where an item is especially valuable or awkward, our fragile packing service and white glove service bring an extra layer of care, from controlled lifts to placement in the destination room.
Bespoke Crating & Soft-Wrapping for Valuable Pieces

For the most fragile and high-value items, blankets and standard cartons are not enough. We build bespoke crates made to the exact dimensions of the piece, with internal battening and foam so the object cannot shift, settle or absorb road vibration during the journey. A gilt-framed mirror, a marble bust or a large canvas each gets a crate designed around its particular weak points rather than forced into a generic box. This is the same standard of protection used when works move between galleries and salerooms, and it is available to private West Sussex clients through our custom crate service.

Soft-wrapping is the layer that sits underneath. We use acid-free tissue against polished and gilded surfaces, then bubble and foam, then an outer blanket or crate. The order of those layers matters: tissue protects the finish, the cushioning absorbs shock, and the crate provides the rigid shell. Corners, feet, handles and any proud carving are padded out so that pressure is never concentrated on a single fragile point. Where pieces are travelling abroad or going into long-term store, our export packing service adds moisture barriers and timber cases built to withstand handling well beyond the back of a removals van.
Working With Dealers, Auction Houses, Estate Agents & Private Collectors

Our antiques work spans the whole West Sussex trade. We collect and deliver for antique dealers who need stock moved between premises, fairs and clients without a scratch; we run consignments to and from auction houses and salerooms against lot lists and deadlines; and we work alongside estate agents clearing or staging period properties. We are recommended by Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, agents who deal regularly with the kind of higher-value homes where antiques and fine art are part of the move.

For private collectors, discretion and reliability are everything. Whether you are downsizing, relocating within the county or sending a single piece to a relative, we treat your collection with the same documented care we give to trade clients. We cover West, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent as standard, plus the wider UK and Europe, so a piece bought at a West Sussex saleroom can be delivered safely wherever it needs to go. Our broader West Sussex removals coverage sits behind all of this, and you can request pricing for any antiques job through our quote request page.

Provenance and condition records protect everyone, which is why a proper condition check and inventory is built into how we handle valuable items. Before we lift a piece, we note and, where helpful, photograph its existing condition — any old splits, chips, losses or restoration — so there is a clear, agreed record of how it left and how it arrived. For dealers and auction houses this dovetails with their own lot documentation; for private collectors it is simple peace of mind that nothing is left open to question.

We also handle the logistics of fairs and salerooms directly. That means timed collections to meet a viewing or vendor deadline, careful loading so heavier and more robust items shield the delicate ones, and deliveries booked in against the receiving department’s requirements. Items are checked in and signed for against the inventory at both ends, so the chain of custody is unbroken from your door to the rostrum or the fair stand. If you are moving a whole collection rather than a single lot, combining this with our specialised antiques moving team keeps one accountable point of contact across the entire job.
faqs: Are you really qualified to move valuable antiques in West Sussex?

Yes. We are a LAPADA member — the Association of Art & Antiques Dealers — which is recognised across the trade, and we are Checkatrade-verified and fully insured with £10m public liability cover. We have moved clocks, period furniture, ceramics, paintings and mirrors for dealers, auction houses and private collectors across West Sussex since 2016. You can see our full credentials on our about us page.
Will my antiques be crated or just wrapped in blankets?

It depends on the piece. Robust items may travel soft-wrapped and blanketed, but for fragile or high-value pieces we build bespoke crates with internal battening and foam so nothing can shift in transit. Acid-free tissue protects polished and gilded surfaces underneath the cushioning. See our custom crate service and fragile packing service for detail.
Do you deliver to and collect from auction houses and antiques fairs?

Yes. We run timed collections and deliveries to and from salerooms and fairs, loaded so the most delicate items are protected and checked against a lot list or inventory at both ends. We cover West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, plus the wider UK and Europe. Request pricing through our quote page.
Do you record the condition of items before moving them?

We do. A condition check and inventory is part of how we handle valuable items — we note and, where helpful, photograph existing condition before lifting, then check items in and sign for them on arrival. This protects collectors and dovetails with dealers’ and auction houses’ own documentation. Pair it with our white glove service for the most careful handling.
